title: "podman run" (and other commands) return "error locating item named "sha256:[...]" for image with ID "[...]": file does not exist
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
```
# podman run -e PORT=8080 -p 8080:8080 boxboat/hello-world-webapp:latest
Error: error locating item named "sha256:e16ad3b2385f4045d00eb3e686e8ab708a5cb282a30866607ec818f8156c44c2" for image with ID "e16ad3b2385f4045d00eb3e686e8ab708a5cb282a30866607ec818f8156c44c2": file does not exist
```
or
```
# podman run boxboat/hello-world-webapp:latest
Error: error locating item named "sha256:e16ad3b2385f4045d00eb3e686e8ab708a5cb282a30866607ec818f8156c44c2" for image with ID "e16ad3b2385f4045d00eb3e686e8ab708a5cb282a30866607ec818f8156c44c2": file does not exist
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Launch Ubuntu 18.04 AMI on AWS
2. Install Podman from Project Atomic PPA
```
cat > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/podman.list <<EOF
deb http://ppa.launchpad.net/projectatomic/ppa/ubuntu xenial main
deb-src http://ppa.launchpad.net/projectatomic/ppa/ubuntu xenial main
EOF

apt-key adv --recv-key --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com 8BECF1637AD8C79D
apt update

apt install podman

curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/projectatomic/registries/master/registries.conf > /etc/containers/registries.conf
```

3. Pull boxboat/hello-world-webapp from dockerhub
```
podman pull boxboat/hello-world-webapp
```

4. Launch container
```
podman run -e PORT=8080 -p 8080:8080 boxboat/hello-world-webapp:latest
Error: error locating item named "sha256:e16ad3b2385f4045d00eb3e686e8ab708a5cb282a30866607ec818f8156c44c2" for image with ID "e16ad3b2385f4045d00eb3e686e8ab708a5cb282a30866607ec818f8156c44c2": file does not exist
```

5. Remove image
```
# podman images
ERRO[0000] error checking if image is a parent "e16ad3b2385f4045d00eb3e686e8ab708a5cb282a30866607ec818f8156c44c2": error locating item named "sha256:e16ad3b2385f4045d00eb3e686e8ab708a5cb282a30866607ec818f8156c44c2" for image with ID "e16ad3b2385f4045d00eb3e686e8ab708a5cb282a30866607ec818f8156c44c2": file does not exist
REPOSITORY                             TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED          SIZE
docker.io/boxboat/hello-world-webapp   latest   e16ad3b2385f   12 minutes ago   6.86 MB
```
```
# podman rmi docker.io/boxboat/hello-world-webapp
Error: error locating item named "sha256:e16ad3b2385f4045d00eb3e686e8ab708a5cb282a30866607ec818f8156c44c2" for image with ID "e16ad3b2385f4045d00eb3e686e8ab708a5cb282a30866607ec818f8156c44c2": file does not exist
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Error: error locating item named "sha256:e16ad3b2385f4045d00eb3e686e8ab708a5cb282a30866607ec818f8156c44c2" for image with ID "e16ad3b2385f4045d00eb3e686e8ab708a5cb282a30866607ec818f8156c44c2": file does not exist
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Container was launched

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
```
# find /var/lib/containers/ -name e16ad3b2385f4045d00eb3e686e8ab708a5cb282a30866607ec818f8156c44c2
/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images/e16ad3b2385f4045d00eb3e686e8ab708a5cb282a30866607ec818f8156c44c2
```

```
-rw------- 1 root root  590 Oct 14 19:50  manifest
```

```
# cat /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images/e16ad3b2385f4045d00eb3e686e8ab708a5cb282a30866607ec818f8156c44c2/manifest
```

```
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

```
# apt list podman
Listing... Done
podman/bionic,now 1.6.1-1~ubuntu18.04~ppa3 amd64 [installed]
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: The config blob is gone, and @raoulbhatia confirmed that it's not there.  I don't have a theory as to where it went, as the image seemed to be pulled without any errors, and the manifest confirms that the image should have a config blob.
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
I will close this as no further info.

This is still happening in podman 1.6.4

 podman images --log-level=debug

podman --version
podman version 1.6.4

**OS** RHEL 8
I'm not sure what is causing this behaviour, but I can attest, that I am observing the same error(s) on my fedora31 install (with podman 1.8.2). How can I remove the images causing the problem?

> I'm not sure what is causing this behaviour, but I can attest, that I am observing the same error(s) on my fedora31 install (with podman 1.8.2). How can I remove the images causing the problem?

if you remove the offending container either by pruning the system (if possible) then deleting the storage contents (e.g. /var/lib/containers/) you can remove the image, however doing so will prune all images. 

Otherwise, I'm not too sure.

My problem is I'm trying to append an existing image and then push/pull it for execution, I'm using the containerregistry repository to do so. 

No matter what I do, podman cannot reference the folder once I pull into podman, docker works fine...

Big problem for those of us trying to use ocp :(
@jimmyherron Append an image? Can you clarify what you mean - are you modifying the image and committing it, or manually editing the image on-disk, or something else?

Hey @mheon 
Let me give a bit more detail.. 

# Background

What I am trying to do is use a jupyter notebook running on kubeflow/Openshift. 

I’m using the latest version of this repository [Kubeflow Fairing](https://github.com/kubeflow/fairing) and for reference, I’m using the ‘append’ builder class.
This class makes use of the Google Container tools [Google/Containerregistry](https://github.com/google/containerregistry). I've also tried replacing the containerregistry supplied with kubeflow with the latest version direct from the repository. 

The append class takes an image from either disk, tarball or repository and adds a layer from a tar ball then updates the manifest, this could be a fs layer or a config layer or often times, both. 


## Investigation 

I've tried many things to resolve the issue, including doing a bit of background investigation as to why this might be happening. 
Below is the results of that investigation from a container that **works in both Docker and Podman.**

Using 'jimmyherron/why:base' from index.docker.io.

This isn't an issue until you couple it with the following information:
After running the append class from google container registry OR if run the whole process using kubeflow-fairing the resulting image, for example, is....
`index.docker.io/jimmyherron/why:44D9D4B4`

When I pull that image, which uses the 'base' image and then adds some data and config to it, it **doesn't work with Podman** and I get the error...

```
ERRO[0000] error checking if image is a parent "669f9cfeb7307f7b67dc6a83f2d94df88789cf27030fea0e3ee4c0a54eea6c33": 
error locating item named "sha256:a9bf31f7f1a1c590addbfcad1c70718865a691dbc672a33ff0cdef956bf8f7f6" for image with ID "a9bf31f7f1a1c590addbfcad1c70718865a691dbc672a33ff0cdef956bf8f7f6": file does not exist
```

Investigating host filesystem for podman in `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images/`
contains: `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-images/a9bf31f7f1a1c590addbfcad1c70718865a691dbc672a33ff0cdef956bf8f7f6`

Seemingly contains the correct folder, in a futile attempt at forcing the issue, I changed the name of folder to `sha256:a9bf31f7f1a1c590addbfcad1c70718865a691dbc672a33ff0cdef956bf8f7f6` including the prefix.
This removes the ERRO[0000] when I run `podman images` etc, but of course the image but it still fails to launch or run. 

However, if I use docker and pull the same `index.docker.io/jimmyherron/why:44D9D4B4` image, it works fine. 

Apologies if I have taken this on a bit of a tangent and it may not be related, but this is what I've come up with after a couple of days of investigation and 'spot the difference' :) Thanks for replying and please let me know if I can provide any more information.

That image should not be even possible to pull:
```console
$ skopeo copy --debug docker://index.docker.io/jimmyherron/why:44D9D4B4 dir:foo
…
DEBU[0153] Writing manifest using preferred type application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json failed: Error writing blob: Size mismatch when copying sha256:0a0523e2ac5db540021a905416b3e926002812f4994948edd1c6d702fbd3d227, expected 16820, got 15527 
FATA[0153] Error writing blob: Size mismatch when copying sha256:0a0523e2ac5db540021a905416b3e926002812f4994948edd1c6d702fbd3d227, expected 16820, got 15527 
```

and indeed, that blob (the config) has size 15527, not 16820 as the manifest claims (and it is not corrupt, the digest matches and it is valid JSON), as can be verified using `skopeo inspect --raw --config`.

Similarly, with a fairly old Podman build:
```
DEBU[0123] Error pulling image ref //jimmyherron/why:44D9D4B4: Error writing blob: blob size mismatch 
  blob size mismatch
ERRO[0123] error pulling image "docker://index.docker.io/jimmyherron/why:44D9D4B4": unable to pull docker://index.docker.io/jimmyherron/why:44D9D4B4: unable to pull image: Error writing blob: blob size mismatch 
```
@mtrmac is this a bug or not?

Looking at the output from `skopeo inspect --raw` on both `docker://jimmyherron/why:base` and `docker://jimmyherron/why:44D9D4B4`, the config blobs for the two images are described as having different digests, which I'd expect, but the same size, which is not what I'd expect, and which we know from `skopeo inspect --raw --config` is incorrect for the derived image's config blob.  To me, that looks like a bug in the tool that built the derived image.

Yes; randomly checking https://github.com/google/containerregistry/blob/8a11dc8c53003ecf5b72ffaf035ba280109356ac/client/v2_2/append_.py#L82-L85 (I have spent _absolutely no time_ to ensure this is actually the code involved in the description above), that code updates the digest but not the size.

Closing.
